compressed_prompt:
I am a software engineer with 7 years of experience, and I am looking for a new job. Can you create a resume for me and explain each section?

Create a professional software engineer resume, detailing each section: contact info, summary, skills, work experience, education, certifications, and projects.
response:

---

## 📖 SECTION-BY-SECTION EXPLANATION

---

### 1. 📬 Contact Information

**What it is:**
Your professional identity and how employers reach you.

**What to include:**
| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| Full Name | Large and prominent at the top |
| Location | City and State only — never full address |
| Phone Number | Professional voicemail set up |
| Email Address | Professional format (firstname.lastname) |
| LinkedIn | Keep profile updated and matching resume |
| GitHub | Active repos demonstrate real skills |
| Portfolio | Optional but highly recommended |

**Key Tips:**
- ✅ Use a professional email — avoid nicknames
- ✅ Make sure your LinkedIn matches your resume
- ✅ Pin your best GitHub projects
- ❌ Never include age, photo, or marital status

---

### 2. 🎯 Professional Summary

**What it is:**
A 3–4 sentence elevator pitch at the top of your resume that immediately tells employers who you are and what value you bring.

**Why it matters:**
Recruiters spend an average of **6–7 seconds** on an initial resume scan. Your summary must hook them immediately.

**The formula:**
```
[Job Title] + [Years of Experience] + [Key Specialization]
+ [1-2 Major Achievements] + [What You Bring to the Team]
```

**What to avoid:**
- ❌ "Hardworking team player who loves to learn" (generic)
- ❌ Listing responsibilities instead of value
- ❌ Making it longer than 4 sentences
- ✅ Always tailor this section to each job description

---

### 3. 🛠️ Technical Skills

**What it is:**
A structured list of your technical competencies, organized by category.

**Why it matters:**
- Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) scan for keywords
- Hiring managers quickly assess technical fit
- Saves space compared to writing skills in paragraph form

**How to organize it:**
```
Languages        → What you code in
Frameworks       → What you build with
Cloud/DevOps     → How you deploy and scale
Databases        → How you store data
Tools/Practices  → How you work
```

**Key Tips:**
- ✅ Only list skills you can confidently discuss in an interview
- ✅ Mirror keywords from the job description
- ✅ Put your strongest skills first within each category
- ❌ Don't include proficiency bars (subjective and meaningless)
- ❌ Don't list outdated or irrelevant technologies

---

### 4. 💼 Work Experience

**What it is:**
The most important section of your resume — a detailed record of your professional history with measurable impact.

**The structure for each role:**
```
Job Title | Company Name | Location | Start Date – End Date
• Achievement bullet 1
• Achievement bullet 2
• Achievement bullet 3
```

**The golden rule — use the CAR formula:**
```
Context  → What was the situation?
Action   → What did YOU specifically do?
Result   → What was the measurable outcome?
```

**Example transformation:**
| ❌ Weak | ✅ Strong |
|---|---|
| "Worked on payment system" | "Redesigned payment processing system, reducing transaction failures by 35%" |
| "Helped improve performance" | "Reduced page load time by 40% through React optimization and lazy loading" |
| "Was part of the team" | "Led a team of 5 engineers to deliver X feature impacting 2M+ users" |

**Quantify everything possible:**
- 💰 Revenue impact
- 📈 Performance improvements (%)
- 👥 Team size led
- 🚀 Scale (users, requests, data)
- ⏱️ Time saved

**Key Tips:**
- ✅ Use strong action verbs: *Led, Architected, Designed, Reduced, Improved, Built*
- ✅ List experience in reverse chronological order (newest first)
- ✅ Focus more bullets on recent/senior roles
- ❌
